steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that some indigenous language expressions appear only in songs, yet current preservation efforts focus exclusively on spoken language. The question asks what the researchers must conclude about what needs to happen next.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — songs use the same phrases as spoken language: the passage says the opposite—some expressions appear only in songs, not in everyday speech. ✗
  • Choice B — songs contain ecological knowledge: the passage doesn't limit the issue to ecological knowledge; the concern is about language expressions broadly. This narrows the claim without support. ✗
  • Choice C — researchers should analyze songs: analysis is not the same as preservation. The passage flags a preservation gap, not a research gap. ✗
  • Choice D — preservation efforts must incorporate songs: directly addresses the gap between where unique expressions live (songs) and where current efforts focus (spoken language). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
